title: Components and JSX
description: CReact components are functions that return JSX. JSX describes resources and processes, not DOM elements.
jsx_intro: CReact uses JSX to describe <0>resources and processes</0>, not DOM elements. <1><WebSite name="blog" /></1> declares that a website named "blog" should exist.
component_functions_intro: Components are plain functions that return JSX. They receive props as their first argument and run once. Reactivity is handled by signals, not re-renders.
fragments_intro: Use <0><>...</></0> to return multiple elements without a wrapper:
reactive_props_intro: Props in CReact are often <0>accessors</0> (getter functions) to preserve reactivity. Components run once; effects re-run when their dependencies change.
tip_access: Use the <0>access()</0> helper to unwrap values that may or may not be accessors: <1>access(props.value)</1> works whether <2>value</2> is <3>5</3> or <4>() => 5</4>.
children_intro: Components can receive children through JSX nesting. Use the <0>children()</0> helper to resolve them reactively.
